beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine while listening to rain on the roof
beats.reasoning: Felix wakes naturally to grey drizzle; yesterday's good-tired mood carries into a slower, reflective morning. Stays home to ease into the day rather than rush out.

beats.activity: Drive to Fremont studio, navigating rain and lighter traffic via local streets
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure is eastbound only; Wallingford to Fremont is a short local commute unaffected. Rain and steady mood suggest an unhurried drive.

beats.activity: Morning tattoo session with first client; rain continues outside studio
beats.reasoning: Steady rain and grey light are perfect for focused studio work; Felix's hyper-focus on clean linework suits this weather. Morning slot aligns with his routine.

beats.activity: Lunch at a cozy nearby spot in Fremont; rain is still steady
beats.reasoning: Rain means no wandering; Felix grabs lunch near the studio rather than driving across town. Keeps the day grounded and minimizes unnecessary movement.

beats.activity: Rest at studio between sessions, reviewing sketches and prepping for afternoon client; Afternoon tattoo session with second client; rain clears
beats.reasoning: Rain ends around 2pm; Felix uses this quiet transition time to reset and focus on the next appointment rather than leaving the studio.

beats.activity: Errand: stop by Elliott Bay Book Company to browse new arrivals
beats.reasoning: Felix can't resist a bookstore; yesterday's foraging find sparked that appetite. Rain has cleared enough for a short drive downtown. Nostalgic bookstore vibe suits his mood.

beats.activity: Drive home to Wallingford; evening light is soft and clear
beats.reasoning: Post-rain Seattle light is beautiful; Felix drives back to Wallingford with a book in hand and the day's good work behind him. Afternoon energy is moderate—ready to wind down.

diary: Woke to that perfect sound of rain drumming on the roof—the kind that makes you want to stay in bed with coffee for an extra hour. Navigated the side streets to Fremont since everyone was losing their minds about the bridge closure. The studio felt cozy with the grey light filtering through the windows. My morning client was great, really committed to the piece we've been planning. Grabbed lunch at the Whole Foods spot nearby; the rain was still going strong and I watched people hurrying past with umbrellas. Afternoon felt slower—I spent time sketching, lost in the work until my second client came in. By then the clouds had finally broken. Ended up in Pioneer Square at Elliott Bay, just browsing for a while. The evening drive back to Wallingford was peaceful with that clear, soft light. Felt like the rain had washed something clean.
thought_process: The weather really shaped today. I usually stress about traffic, but the bridge closure meant fewer cars on the local streets—felt like a gift. I leaned into the slow, grey mood instead of fighting it. The rain kept people indoors or moving with purpose, which made for better focus in the studio. Both clients were engaged and present, which always makes the work feel more meaningful. I chose to go to Elliott Bay instead of heading straight home because I needed that decompression time, that space between work and evening. The book browsing felt like a small ritual to mark the day's transition. By the time I drove home, my mood had shifted from cozy-grey to calm-clear.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
